title: Pull IPs out of text
prompt: Write extract_ips(text) that returns a list of the valid IPv4 addresses found in the space-separated text, in order. A valid IP has 4 numeric parts, each 0-255.

help.plain: Scan the words in the text and keep the ones that are real IP addresses.
help.analogy: Like panning for gold — most words wash away, you keep the ones shaped like an IP with valid numbers.
help.nudge: For each word: split on '.', and check there are 4 parts that are all digits in range. `999` should fail the range check.

details.restate: Find every valid IPv4 address in a space-separated string and return them in order. 'Valid' means 4 dot-separated numbers, each between 0 and 255.

io.because: 999 is out of range, so that token isn't a valid IP

terms.meaning: an address like 10.0.0.1 — four numbers 0-255 separated by dots
